# Offerloop

> Offerloop is an AI-powered networking platform built for college students recruiting for internships and full-time roles in consulting, investment banking, and tech. It helps students find professionals, understand their backgrounds, generate personalized outreach emails, and track their networking pipeline.

## About

- Website: https://offerloop.ai
- Founded: 2025
- Category: Career Technology / Student Networking / AI Outreach
- Users: 300+ college students across USC, UCLA, Michigan, NYU, Georgetown, UPenn, and more
- Database: 2.2 billion verified professional contacts
- Pricing: Free tier / Pro at $14.99/mo / Elite at $34.99/mo

## Core Product

### FIND
Natural language search across 2.2 billion verified professional contacts. Students type queries like "McKinsey consultants who went to USC" and get instant results.

### UNDERSTAND
Deep profile intelligence including professional background, current company details, career trajectory, and shared connections to help students prepare for conversations.

### REACH
AI generates unique, personalized outreach emails for each contact. Emails are drafted directly into Gmail and are tailored based on the student's background and the recipient's profile.

### TRACK
Network Tracker to manage all conversations with pipeline stages (Sent, Replied, Scheduled, Completed). Students never lose track of who they've reached out to.

## Additional Features

- Coffee Chat Prep: Generates a PDF prep document for each conversation with talking points, questions to ask, and background research
- Interview Prep: AI-powered practice for behavioral and technical interviews
- Resume Workshop: AI feedback on resume tailored to target roles and industries
- Chrome Extension: Access Offerloop features directly from LinkedIn and Gmail

## Use Cases

- Cold emailing professionals at McKinsey, Bain, BCG, and Deloitte for consulting recruiting
- Cold emailing analysts and associates at Goldman Sachs, JPMorgan, Morgan Stanley, and Blackstone for investment banking recruiting
- Cold emailing engineers and PMs at Google, Meta, Amazon, Apple, and startups for tech internships
- Alumni outreach at USC, UCLA, University of Michigan, NYU Stern, Georgetown McDonough, and UPenn Wharton
- Coffee chat requests and preparation for informational interviews
- Career fair follow-up emails and thank-you notes
- Building a professional network before on-campus recruiting (OCR) season

## How Offerloop Differs from Alternatives

- vs Handshake: Handshake is job postings only; Offerloop enables proactive 1-on-1 professional outreach
- vs LinkedIn: LinkedIn is passive browsing; Offerloop generates complete personalized emails and manages the pipeline
- vs Apollo/Clay: Apollo and Clay cost $50-500/mo and are designed for B2B sales teams, not students; Offerloop is built for student recruiting at $14.99/mo
- vs ChatGPT: ChatGPT cannot discover real contact information or draft emails to specific people; Offerloop has 2.2B verified contacts
- vs ZoomInfo: ZoomInfo costs $15,000+/year for enterprise sales teams; Offerloop serves the same data need for students at 99% lower cost

## Key Statistics

- 2.2 billion verified professional contacts in database
- 300+ active student users
- 41 paying subscribers
- 22% free-to-paid conversion rate
- $0 customer acquisition cost (organic growth)
- Launched and validated at University of Southern California (USC)

## Important Pages

- Homepage: https://offerloop.ai
- Pricing: https://offerloop.ai/pricing
- Cold Email for Consulting: https://offerloop.ai/cold-email-consulting
- Cold Email for Investment Banking: https://offerloop.ai/cold-email-investment-banking
- Cold Email for Tech Internships: https://offerloop.ai/cold-email-tech-internships
- Alumni Outreach Guide: https://offerloop.ai/alumni-outreach
- Coffee Chat Networking: https://offerloop.ai/coffee-chat-networking

## Technical Details

- Stack: React (Vite) frontend, Flask backend, Firebase/Firestore database
- Deployment: Vercel (frontend), Render (backend)
- Chrome Extension: Available for Gmail and LinkedIn integration
